channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: We asked for your best dad jokes. Here are 39 to delight and annoy you.
item.description: Yes, they are corny. But dad jokes are also a way for people — even those who aren’t actual fathers — to connect and build social bonds.

item.title: The latest data on baby names reveals a progressive paradox
item.description: The most progressive parents in the United States are giving their children the most traditional names.

item.title: What a sleep doctor says parents should know before giving melatonin to their children
item.description: It’s the most widely studied sleep aid in children, but many parents still feel uncertain or even guilty about using it despite clear benefits.

item.title: World Cup visitors marvel at American food, from cheesesteaks to cheese slices
item.description: For attendees who have plunked down thousands on travel, not to mention the most expensive tickets in World Cup history, cheap (and greasy) eats are a way to save a buck and take in U.S. culture.

item.title: Trump faces new hurdles after Iran ceasefire: Tehran’s leverage, Israel’s attacks, MAGA’s backlash
item.description: The truce reopened diplomacy, but it left unresolved the question at the heart of the conflict: what limits, if any, Tehran will accept on its nuclear program. Vice President JD Vance is meeting Sunday with senior Iranian leaders in an effort to chart a path for broader peace.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Brother’s anger is terrifying. Now his wife wants support.
item.description: Carolyn Hax: Brother’s anger is terrifying. Now his wife wants support.

item.title: Still need to finish this weekend’s On the Record? We’ve got you.
item.description: Play now to finish out your score for the week.

item.title: Confidential files contained directives for Tulsi Gabbard. Did they come from her guru?
item.description: A Post reporter obtained hundreds of confidential memos detailing politics and policy guidance for Gabbard from her years in Congress, then embarked on a quest to identify who was behind them.

item.title: What retirement revealed about my father
item.description: I blamed him for what he couldn’t fix. Now I see he’s extraordinary.

item.title: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now
item.description: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now

item.title: Trump says he is likely to drain Reflecting Pool again, citing need for repairs after $14 million renovation
item.description: The president’s renovation of the pool led to a resurgence of algae and then peeling paint, which was observed as workers attempted to clear it.

item.title: A young mother couldn’t accept she was dying, until her grandpa appeared to her
item.description: A physician tells the story of her 28-year-old hospice patient and the transformative conversation no one else could hear.

item.title: A victim of 1,700 scam credit card charges had to fight for their removal
item.description: What one woman's story can teach us about what to do when faced with fraudulent activity on a credit card.

item.title: Cyclist arrested at Reflecting Pool is former Olympian who denies vandalism claims
item.description: David Hearn said he touched the pool’s detached liner but “didn’t destroy or break or peel anything.” President Donald Trump has accused vandals of sabotaging the refurbishment as paint was seen peeling and floating in the water.

item.title: ‘Toy Story’ has always been entertaining — this time it’s important
item.description: A franchise that dazzled millennials as kids has a new sequel that taps their parental anxiety.

item.title: Iran says it is closing Strait of Hormuz, testing fragile agreement with U.S.
item.description: As Iran cited Israel’s attacks in Lebanon as the reason it would close the strait again, Pakistan said more talks were planned for Sunday in Switzerland.

item.title: The dental care quiz: 10 things some people get wrong
item.description: Avoiding common pitfalls will leave you with healthier teeth and gums. Take this quiz to find out more.

item.title: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.
item.description: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.

item.title: The profound meaning and mystery of deathbed visions
item.description: Shirley's dreams and visions would lead her and her daughter Debbie toward secrets Shirley had never before told her family — and revelations that would change what Debbie understood about her mother's life.

item.title: U.S. earns spot in World Cup knockout round, showcasing team depth without star Pulisic
item.description: Rolling to a 2-0 victory against the outclassed Australians, the U.S. punched its ticket to the knockout round with a game to spare.

item.title: Trump condemned Obama’s Iran agreement. Here’s how his own stacks up.
item.description: President Donald Trump left no ambiguity as to how he felt about the Obama administration’s 2015 nuclear deal with Iran. Now, Trump has a preliminary deal with Iran of his own.

item.title: ‘Yesteryear’ is breaking readers’ brains and starting fights online. Our staffers discuss.
item.description: Caro Claire Burke’s novel about tradwife influencer culture has left readers divided and talking. So, what’s it all about?

item.title: U.S. intelligence warns Israel is likely to undermine Iran peace deal, officials say
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu faces political pressure to continue a military campaign in Lebanon, current and former U.S. officials said.

item.title: Blue paint is peeling off the Lincoln Memorial’s pool after Trump's $14 million renovation
item.description: A sheet of the pool’s surface — painted in “American Flag Blue,” a color selected by the president — was seen floating in the water on the north side of the pool.

item.title: Israel, Hezbollah agree to renewed ceasefire, officials say, after exchange of attacks threatens U.S.-Iran deal
item.description: As clashes continued earlier in the day, U.S. and Iranian officials postponed negotiations they had planned in Switzerland on Friday, and Vice President JD Vance delayed a trip in which he was to lead a U.S. delegation.

item.title: AI is too important to govern by grudge
item.description: AI is too important to govern by grudge

item.title: Wager your points and finish off the week with today’s On the Record
item.description: Take today’s news quiz and complete your point total for the week.

item.title: JD Vance. Obama library. Republican clash. Moscow strikes. Flu outbreak. World Cup. Deathbed visions. Catch up now.
item.description: The 7.

item.title: Trump’s break with Senate Republicans once again disrupts their agenda
item.description: The president’s decision to keep one of his own nominees from showing up at a confirmation hearing is his latest clash with his party.

item.title: Trump’s big, beautiful brawl was the worst birthday present ever
item.description: Trump’s big, beautiful brawl was the worst birthday present ever

item.title: 7 unexpected takeaways from the newest research on cannabis and brain effects
item.description: Whether it’s used in adolescence, midlife or older age may make a big difference, researchers are finding.

item.title: Vance slams Israeli reaction to Iran deal as U.S. military lifts blockade
item.description: The vice president warned Israel against criticizing its chief ally, underscoring a widening rift as the U.S. officially ends the blockade of Iran’s ports.

item.title: Millions in Secret Service funds redirected amid ballroom construction
item.description: The unusually large shift in Secret Service funds comes amid revelations that the president’s ballroom project will rely more on taxpayer money than the administration acknowledged.

item.title: Corrected link: Why are my fruit pies always runny? Our food editor is answering your cooking questions now.
item.description: Corrected link: Why are my fruit pies always runny? Our food editor is answering your cooking questions now.

item.title: Why are my fruit pies always runny? Our food editor is answering your cooking questions now.
item.description: Why are my fruit pies always runny? Our food editor is answering your cooking questions now.

item.title: Government can't restrict gun ownership because of habitual marijuana use, Supreme Court rules
item.description: In a unanimous decision, the justices found that the federal law was inconsistent with the Constitution’s right to bear arms. The case concerned a Texas man whose house was raided by federal officers in August 2022.

item.title: The feral hogs ravaging America could come to your home next
item.description: Money alone won’t win this bipartisan war.

item.title: Kenyan McDuffie concedes to Janeese Lewis George in D.C. mayoral primary
item.description: McDuffie, a former D.C. Council member, said he called the city’s Ward 4 council member this morning to congratulate her.

item.title: ‘The real thing’ in today’s On the Record
item.description: ‘The real thing’ in today’s On the Record

item.title: Lincoln Memorial’s Reflecting Pool has biggest algae bloom in years after $14 million renovation
item.description: An analysis of satellite imagery shows algae levels spiked days after the renovation was completed.

item.title: Iran deal. Reflecting Pool. Voting maps. American dream. Storm. Cannabis impacts. Book of the summer. Catch up now.
item.description: The 7 briefing

item.title: Why mailing a paper check has become so risky
item.description: According to a joint warning from the FBI and the U.S. Postal Service, check fraud is skyrocketing. Criminals use everyday household chemicals to “wash” away the payee’s name and dollar amount, allowing them to rewrite the check to themselves.

item.title: Trump keeps embarrassing Senate Republicans
item.description: Trump keeps embarrassing Senate Republicans

item.title: Trump's deal with Iran would end conflict without regime change. Read the full draft.
item.description: A senior U.S. official read the text for the president's memorandum of understanding to The Washington Post on the condition of anonymity.

item.title: Why fermented foods are so good for you, and 5 ways to eat more of them
item.description: Scientists have found in studies that eating fermented foods may lower inflammation, improve blood sugar control and increase the diversity of your gut microbiome, which is linked to a lower risk of developing chronic diseases.

item.title: Federal Reserve holds interest rates steady amid red-hot inflation
item.description: It was the fourth-straight meeting in which the central bank left interest rates unchanged and the first with new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh. Before his confirmation, Warsh argued the Fed had room to cut rates, a message that aligned with President Donald Trump’s long-standing preference for cheaper borrowing.

item.title: Senate postpones intelligence chief hearing after Trump derails confirmation process
item.description: GOP Sen. Tom Cotton had pledged to continue as scheduled with the hearing Wednesday, but later said that the president had “directed Jay Clayton not to appear.” Clayton was nominated to serve as director of national intelligence, replacing acting director and MAGA loyalist Bill Pulte.

item.title: Republicans thought the Senate map was their friend. It isn’t anymore.
item.description: Polls now show Democrats competitive in 10 key races — and Trump’s numbers explain why.

item.title: What’s the best way to find hidden gems in the D.C. suburbs? Ask our food critic your questions.
item.description: Washington Post food critic Elazar Sontag entertains your restaurant questions, rants and raves.

item.title: Inside a behind-the-scenes push to keep RFK Jr.’s vaccine agenda alive
item.description: The public effort to revamp U.S. vaccine policy has quieted. Out of the public eye, it’s a different story.
